context.projectOverview: The standalone plan command writes only .bober/specs/*.json; the spec-to-contracts materialization lives solely inside the run pipeline, so sprint errors with 'No sprint contracts found' even though plan prints 'Next: sprint'. This plan extracts a single shared materializeContracts helper used by both plan and run, has plan eagerly materialize contracts (preferring the planner's embedded spec.sprints with feature-derived fallback), gives materialized contracts deterministic zero-padded ids, and hardens the sprint command against the failure modes eager materialization amplifies.

completedSprints.title: Extract shared deterministic materializeContracts helper
completedSprints.summary: Created src/orchestrator/contract-materialization.ts (materializeContracts feature-derived branch, deterministic zero-padded sprint-<specId>-NN ids); pipeline.ts:853 delegates to it. Commit 1a7cd2b.

contract.title: Embedded-sprint materialization and eager wiring into plan
contract.description: Extend materializeContracts to prefer valid embedded spec.sprints: safeParse each entry against SprintContractSchema, normalize status to proposed, and on any parse or precision failure fall back to feature-derived generation without throwing. Wire runPlanCommand to call materializeContracts after a successful non-clarification plan, clearing the current specId's prior contracts first, and correct the next-step hint so it points to a command that now functions.

successCriteria.description: Running npm run build and the typecheck completes with zero errors after wiring eager materialization into the plan command.

Given a spec whose sprints array entries each satisfy SprintContractSchema, materializeContracts writes one contract file per entry with status normalized to proposed and the embedded success criteria and precision fields preserved.

Given a spec whose sprints entries omit status or use an unknown verificationMethod, materializeContracts does not throw and instead writes feature-derived contracts that satisfy SprintContractSchema.
After runPlanCommand completes for a ready spec the contracts directory contains schema-valid files, while a spec with status needs-clarification yields zero contract files.
Re-running materialization for the same specId overwrites that specId's prior contracts and leaves no stale higher-numbered files behind.
The plan command prints a next-step hint that points to a command which succeeds, consistent with the hint printed by plan answer.

nonGoals: Do not add a separate contracts CLI command.
Do not modify the sprint command's contract selection in this sprint; that is Sprint 3.
stopConditions: Stop when plan writes valid contracts from embedded sprints with feature-derived fallback and the needs-clarification path writes none.
Stop when re-planning the same spec does not accumulate stale contract files.
contract.definitionOfDone: The plan command eagerly materializes schema-valid sprint contracts from embedded spec.sprints when present, falls back to feature-derived generation otherwise, skips materialization for needs-clarification specs, avoids stale-file accumulation, and prints an accurate next-step hint.
assumptions: Embedded entries that fail safeParse trigger a whole-set feature-derived fallback rather than a partial mix, keeping a single id scheme per spec.
Clearing prior contracts is scoped to files whose specId matches the current spec, leaving other specs untouched.
outOfScope: Honoring embedded sprints inside run is already delivered via the shared helper from Sprint 1 and needs no extra plan-side work.
contract.generatorNotes: Add the embedded branch to materializeContracts from Sprint 1: iterate spec.sprints, SprintContractSchema.safeParse each, override id with deterministic sprint-<specId>-NN, force status to proposed, and pass through saveContract (which still enforces the precision gate). If any entry fails to parse, log and fall back to the feature-derived branch for the whole spec. In runPlanCommand (plan.ts), after printPlan and only when result.kind is not needs-clarification, call materializeContracts(spec, projectRoot, config). Before writing, delete existing .bober/contracts files whose parsed specId equals spec.specId. Update printPlan's final hint; reconcile the inconsistency where plan suggests sprint (plan.ts:171) but plan answer suggests run (plan.ts:290).
contract.evaluatorNotes: Test three paths: valid embedded sprints used verbatim with normalized status; malformed embedded sprints falling back to feature-derived without throwing; needs-clarification spec producing zero contracts. Verify idempotency by materializing a 3-sprint spec, then a 2-sprint version of the same specId, and asserting only two files remain. Confirm the printed hint names a command that the new code makes functional.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
